---
title: "Appendix"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

## Reproducibility notes

The project is designed to run from a clean environment with Python 3.11+. The main analysis script downloads the public LUAD RNA-seq matrix, LUAD clinical matrix, and TCGA survival table from UCSC Xena endpoints, caches those files locally, and writes all downstream outputs into the `results/` directory.

## Command-line workflow

```bash
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
quarto render
```

If you want to force a fresh data download, run:

```bash
python -m src.analysis --force-download
```

## Output files

After a successful render, the key outputs are:

- `results/tables/sample_summary.csv`
- `results/tables/top_differential_expression.csv`
- `results/tables/classifier_summary.csv`
- `results/tables/top_biomarker.csv`
- `results/figures/pca.png`
- `results/figures/volcano.png`
- `results/figures/heatmap_top_genes.png`
- `results/figures/classifier_roc.png`
- `results/figures/kaplan_meier_top_biomarker.png`

## Extension ideas

The project can be strengthened further by adding one or more of the following:

1. validation in an external LUAD cohort from GEO;
2. integration of mutation or copy-number data;
3. gene-set scoring at the sample level;
4. consensus feature selection across several classifier families;
5. multi-omic follow-up using CPTAC LUAD proteomics.
